title: OpenCode Desktop App — Coding Pattern Map

content.framework_version: solid-js (no specific version detected in imports)
content.ui_library: @opencode-ai/ui — provides Collapsible, FileIcon, Icon, Dialog, Toast, and createSimpleContext
content.styling_system: Tailwind CSS via class strings (no CSS modules except codebase-map.css)
content.state_management: SolidJS signals (createSignal, createMemo), stores (createStore), and context (createSimpleContext)

examples: inspector-page.tsx:9 — export function InspectorPage()
session-list.tsx:4 — export function SessionList()
event-detail-drawer.tsx:4 — export function EventDetailDrawer()
timeline-table.tsx:51 — export function TimelineTable()
timeline-filters.tsx:23 — export function TimelineFilters()
activity-feed.tsx:34 — export function ActivityFeed()
viz-panel.tsx:6 — export function VizPanel()
session-badge.tsx:11 — export function SessionBadges()
codebase-map.tsx:62 — export function CodebaseMap()

component_signatures.pattern: export const ComponentName: Component = () => { /* arrow component */ }
examples: export-button.tsx:19 — export const ExportDebugButton: Component = () => {
export-button.tsx:120 — export const DialogExportDebug: Component = () => {

component_signatures.pattern: function HelperComponent(props: { prop1: type; prop2: type }) { /* inline helper */ }
examples: event-detail-drawer.tsx:185 — function FieldRow(props: { label: string; value: string })
event-detail-drawer.tsx:194 — function JsonFields(props: { data: unknown; depth: number })
inspector-page.tsx:76 — function ConnectedBar(props: { connected: boolean })
timeline-table.tsx:147 — function EventRow(props: { event: RuntimeEvent; isSelected: boolean; onClick: () => void })

content.props_convention: Simple inline object types for props — no separate Props interface unless exported. Props are destructured via dot notation on `props.xxx`, NOT destructured at the parameter level.

content.usage: export const { use: useHookName, provider: ProviderName } = createSimpleContext({ name, gate, init })
examples: inspector.tsx:207 — export const { use: useInspector, provider: InspectorProvider } = createSimpleContext({ name: "Inspector", gate: false, init: () => { ... } })
session-viz.tsx:159 — export const { use: useSessionViz, provider: SessionVizProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
file.tsx:52 — export const { use: useFile, provider: FileProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
settings.tsx:161 — export const { use: useSettings, provider: SettingsProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
language.tsx:201 — export const { use: useLanguage, provider: LanguageProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
platform.tsx:126 — export const { use: usePlatform, provider: PlatformProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
sdk.tsx:4 — export const { use: useSDK, provider: SDKProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
server-sdk.tsx:247 — export const { use: useServerSDK, provider: ServerSDKProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
highlights.tsx:139 — export const { use: useHighlights, provider: HighlightsProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
layout.tsx:136 — export const { use: useLayout, provider: LayoutProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
command.tsx:259 — export const { use: useCommand, provider: CommandProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
notification.tsx:108 — export const { use: useNotification, provider: NotificationProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
permission.tsx:47 — export const { use: usePermission, provider: PermissionProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
sync.tsx:450 — export const { use: useServerSync, provider: ServerSyncProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
local.tsx:54 — export const { use: useLocal, provider: LocalProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
servers.tsx:5 — export const { use: useServers, provider: ServersProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
comments.tsx:198 — export const { use: useComments, provider: CommentsProvider } = createSimpleContext({ ... })
content.consumption_pattern: const { sessions, events, filters, setFilter } = useInspector()  // destructured at top of component
content.provider_wrapping: Context providers wrap the component tree at page level: <InspectorProvider><InspectorShell /></InspectorProvider>

examples: inspector-page.tsx:20 — const [showSidebar, setShowSidebar] = createSignal(true)
event-detail-drawer.tsx:6 — const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = createSignal<'fields' | 'raw'>('fields')
export-button.tsx:29 — const [exporting, setExporting] = createSignal(false)
timeline-table.tsx:56 — const [scrollTop, setScrollTop] = createSignal(0)
timeline-table.tsx:57 — const [containerHeight, setContainerHeight] = createSignal(600)
activity-feed.tsx:38 — const [autoScroll, setAutoScroll] = createSignal(true)

createMemo_usage.pattern: const derived = createMemo(() => { /* compute from signals */ })
examples: session-list.tsx:7 — const sessionMeta = createMemo(() => { ... counts from events ... })
session-list.tsx:19 — const sortedSessions = createMemo(() => { ... sort sessions ... })
event-detail-drawer.tsx:10 — const children = createMemo(() => getChildren(event().id))
event-detail-drawer.tsx:16 — const parent = createMemo(() => getParent(event().id))
timeline-table.tsx:58 — const totalHeight = createMemo(() => filteredEvents().length * ROW_HEIGHT)
timeline-table.tsx:60 — const visibleRange = createMemo(() => { ... virtual scroll calc ... })
activity-feed.tsx:42 — const currentAgentActivity = createMemo(() => { ... active session heartbeat ... })
inspector.tsx:264 — const filteredEvents = createMemo(() => { ... filter logic ... })
inspector.tsx:288 — const stats = createMemo((): InspectorStats => { ... stats calc ... })

createEffect_usage.pattern: createEffect(() => { /* side effect that reacts to signal changes */ })
examples: activity-feed.tsx:73 — createEffect(() => { items = activity(); auto-scroll logic })
codebase-map.tsx:68 — createEffect(() => { claimed = claimedPaths(); update active packages })
highlights.tsx:155 — createEffect(() => { if (state.started) return; init logic })
file-tree.tsx:215 — createEffect with `on` for dependency control
inspector.tsx:210 — const [store, setStore] = createStore<InspectorStore>({ events: [], filters: { ... }, ... })
session-viz.tsx:163 — const [sessions, setSessions] = createStore<Record<string, SessionVizSession>>({})
highlights.tsx:143 — const [store, setStore, _, ready] = persisted('highlights.v1', createStore<Store>({ ... }))
settings.tsx:162 — const [store, setStore] = createStore<Settings>({ ... }) layered with persisted()
file.tsx:66 — createStore for file tree state

createStore_usage.pattern: setStore with produce() for immutable updates
createStore_usage.notes: produce from solid-js/store for immer-like mutable syntax with immutable updates

content.signal_accessor_convention: Signals are called as functions (e.g. `events()`) to read, NOT as `.value`. This is SolidJS standard.

content.primary: Tailwind CSS utility classes via `class="..."` attribute
examples: class="flex flex-col h-full"
class="px-2 py-1.5 border-b border-border"
class="text-[11px] font-medium text-text"
class={`px-2 py-0.5 text-[10px] rounded border transition-colors ${condition ? 'bg-red-500/20 text-red-400' : 'bg-transparent text-text-muted'}`}
class={`w-full flex items-start gap-1.5 px-2 py-1.5 text-left transition-colors hover:bg-background-menu ${isActive ? 'bg-background-menu border-l-2 border-text' : 'border-l-2 border-transparent'}`}
content.inline_styles: Used sparingly for dynamic CSS custom properties:
examples: style={{ 'border-color': statusColor(), color: statusColor(), 'background-color': `${statusColor()}15` }}
style={{ height: `${totalHeight()}px`, position: 'relative' }}
style={{ transform: `translateY(${offsetY()}px)` }}
style={{ 'background-color': `${session.color}15` }}
content.css_module: Only one instance — codebase-map.css imported via `import './codebase-map.css'` in codebase-map.tsx:3
content.css_classes_for_colors: Custom color tokens: text-text, text-text-muted, text-text-weak, bg-background-base, bg-background-element, bg-background-menu, border-border, border-border-weaker, border-border-weaker-base
content.spacing_pattern: Small components use [9px], [10px], [11px], xs, sm. Consistent use of px-2 py-1.5 for list items, px-3 py-2 for headers.
content.button_patterns: Buttons use `px-1.5 py-0.5 text-[10px] rounded border transition-colors` with hover states

event_streaming.pattern: SSE or WebSocket connection via useServerSDK, processed in context init

content.fetch_usage: Raw fetch via platform.fetch or global fetch (highlights.tsx:173)
content.async_handler_pattern: async function handleAction() { try { ... } catch (err) { ... } finally { setState(false) } }
content.example: export-button.tsx:35 — const handleExport = async () => { setExporting(true); try { ... } catch { showToast error } finally { setExporting(false) } }
content.createResource: Used once in language.tsx for lazy locale loading: createResource(() => locale(), loadLocaleDict)

content.ref_pattern: let containerRef: HTMLDivElement | undefined (activity-feed.tsx:39), then ref={containerRef} in JSX

content.exports: Default export for main component (file-tree.tsx), named exports for everything else
content.inline_helpers: Helper functions defined below the main export in the same file (formatTime, formatDuration, EventRow in timeline-table.tsx)
content.types_collocation: Types are exported from context files alongside the context provider (inspector.tsx exports RuntimeEvent, EventStatus, EventCategory, etc.)
content.types_in_types_dir: Only 2 type files in types/ — mcp.ts and agent.ts — for cross-cutting types not tied to a specific context
content.hooks_dir: Single hooks file: use-providers.ts. Most 'hooks' are really context accessor functions returned by createSimpleContext
content.local_stylesheet: Only 1 local CSS file (codebase-map.css); everything else is Tailwind
content.constant_files: Constant types and lookup tables live at top of context file (EVENT_CATEGORIES, EVENT_CATEGORY_LABELS, EVENT_STATUS_COLORS in inspector.tsx)
content.virtual_scroll: Homemade virtual scroll in timeline-table.tsx using createMemo, onScroll, and translateY — no external library
content.map_method_in_jsx: sessions().slice(0, 1).map(...) used in viz-panel.tsx:42 — mixing .map() in JSX alongside <For>
content.fragment_usage: <Show when={x}>{(ev) => <>{/* content */}</>}</Show> — using Show's callback for scoped access
content.empty_state_pattern: Components consistently handle empty/loading/error states via <Show when={condition} fallback={<EmptyState />}>
content.i18n: language.t('key') pattern used only in export-button.tsx; most components use hardcoded English strings

content.test_files: file-tree.test.ts found (no other tests in these directories)
content.convention: Tests live next to source files or in packages/app/src/

content: 1. No `hooks/` or `types/` directories at expected paths — smart_find returns empty files list despite directories existing (likely symlinks or permission issue)
2. createSimpleContext is universally used (17+ contexts) but its definition is in @opencode-ai/ui/context (external package) — not in this repo
3. CSS module import pattern used only once (codebase-map.css) — anomaly within Tailwind-first codebase
4. export-button.tsx imports useDialog from '@opencode-ai/ui/context/dialog' but uses it as a raw function rather than SolidJS context — pattern inconsistency
5. viz-panel.tsx uses array .map() in JSX (line 42) alongside <For> component — mixing two iteration patterns
6. file-tree.tsx uses splitProps, Dynamic, createEffect with `on`, untrack — advanced SolidJS patterns not seen in inspector/session-viz components
7. No actual `hooks` directory content — the only hook file (use-providers.ts) is effectively a thin wrapper around server-sync context

summary: The OpenCode desktop app is a SolidJS application with a context-driven architecture. Components are simple functions that consume context via useXxx() hooks returned by createSimpleContext. Styling is Tailwind CSS exclusively (one CSS module anomaly). State management uses createSignal for local state, createStore for complex state, createMemo for derived data, and createEffect for side effects. Types are co-located with their context providers rather than in a central types/ directory. The component shape follows a consistent pattern: no props interface for page-level components, inline object types for helper sub-components, and dot-property access on props.
